title: cuphea jorullensis
intro: Cuphea jorullensis, also known as the 'Cigar Plant', is a flowering plant species within the Lythraceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used in folk medicine, showcasing antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cuphea jorullensis include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support digestive health and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Cuphea jorullensis can be found in the form of tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate various health conditions.
